policy: This file is the perf contract, enforced by bench/gate.py (run via `make check`).
Every metric under `gates` has a `ceiling` — the regression bar, set from quiet-
machine measurements plus noise headroom — and cold corpora also carry `budget`,
the absolute product budget from the README. Exceeding either fails the gate.
Raising a ceiling is a deliberate, reviewed act: the same change MUST append an
entry to `revisions` (date, reason, what moved and why the cost is justified);
gate.py fails any ceiling raise that arrives without a new revisions entry.
Lowering a ceiling needs no ceremony. `reference` numbers are informational
(warm/huge paths have no automated harness yet) and are not machine-checked.
method: cold: process start → MTLDrawable presented handler (glass time), p50 of n=10 via bench/bench.sh; scroll: 480 fully serialized encode+GPU frames over large.md (VW_SCROLL_BENCH=1), p95

scroll_frame_large_5mb_ms.note: 480 frames, fully serialized encode+GPU per frame (pessimistic), scrollbar included, 65 of 35344 blocks resident

notes: All numbers measured with selection structures, appearance reactivity, scrollbar, and async syntax highlighting active — no bench-only fast paths.
Cold p95 excludes the one-time Gatekeeper scan of a freshly signed binary (~500-900ms first launch of a new build).
Documents >1MB open via the first-paint slice (fence-aware ≤256KB sync parse); the full parse splices in on a background task with viewport anchoring.
Benchmark on a quiet machine: p50 of 10 runs shrugs off one or two noisy launches, but sustained CPU load (builds, agent fleets) inflates every phase and will trip ceilings a quiet re-run passes.

revisions.reason: Initial machine-checked gate, landed with the mermaid-diagram feature. Cold ceilings set from quiet-machine p50s (~165-195ms across corpora, including WebKit now linked — pre-main unchanged at ~6.2ms) with ~1.4x noise headroom; scroll ceiling is the 120Hz frame budget the in-app bench already enforces. mermaid.md corpus added to pin that diagram work (skeleton layout, raster requests) stays off the first-paint path.
